target.title: fix(qqbot): allow scoped sandbox media sends
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where sandbox-enabled agents could create a media file in their workspace, then fail to send it through QQBot when the reply used the sandbox-visible `/workspace/...` path.

This affects the QQBot media delivery workflow for sandboxed agent replies, including `<qqmedia>` block replies, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payloads, and host-read media loading.

## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for first-pass review. The current update has fresh QQBot/Core regression tests, type checking, near-real gateway/message-delivery proof for the `/workspace/...` path, a redacted live QQBot C2C media-tag proof, and a passing ClawSweeper commit review.
- Root cause: sandboxed agents see generated files under the virtual root `/workspace`, but the remaining QQBot media paths still treated `/workspace/...` as an absolute host path instead of resolving that virtual root through the scoped agent workspace. That left gateway `<qqmedia>` block replies, structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media paths, and host-read media loading out of sync with the scoped workspace contract.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the fix maps only the sandbox virtual root `/workspace` to the already configured `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` before local media validation or host-read loading. This repairs the source invariant shared by the affected QQBot media entry points: sandbox-visible workspace paths must resolve to the same scoped host workspace that the session authorized.
- Patch quality notes: the additional host-read error handling and `expect.any(...)` assertions are inherited from the existing PR diff and are covered by the focused tests. This update does not add a new fallback root or bypass validation; it preserves the existing scoped local-root checks, adds a traversal regression for `/workspace/../outside-report.docx`, and now preserves already-authorized host `/workspace/...` paths before falling back to sandbox virtual-root mapping. Maintainer feedback on QQBot media skill wording is addressed by making `<qqmedia>{实际路径或URL}</qqmedia>` the generic guidance and documenting `/workspace/...` only as a sandbox-visible path source.

## Linked context

- Related: #92827
- PR rescue target: #92872
- Behavior or issue addressed: sandbox-enabled agents can generate media files in their workspace and send them through QQBot using `/workspace/...` paths in block replies or structured media payloads.
- Why it matters / User impact: users can ask a sandboxed agent to create a document, image, voice file, video, or other supported file and send it through QQBot without the file being rejected only because the agent referenced the sandbox path it can see.
- What did NOT change: this does not add new QQBot upload roots, does not relax scoped media roots, does not change file-size limits, does not add a new public API, and does not claim live QQ group delivery coverage. The live external proof used QQBot C2C because that was the available authorized session.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: the source of truth remains the session-scoped `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`, `mediaAccess.localRoots`, scoped media roots, and OpenClaw media directory. `/workspace` is treated as a virtual alias for the current agent workspace only; all non-workspace absolute paths keep existing behavior.
- Related open PR / same-contract scan: this is an update to the existing linked PR for #92827, not a competing PR. The change stays inside the current QQBot sandbox media contract already under review in #92872.
- Out of scope: no schema/config migration, no new provider/channel contract, no permission broadening for arbitrary host paths, no live external QQ group proof, and no unrelated channel changes.

## Real behavior proof

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Validation `qqbot-gateway-media-focused-postcommit` passed on the submitted commit. A separate redacted live QQBot C2C proof also sent the sandbox-generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt` through the high-level `<qqmedia>` tag path.

  ```text
  [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-messaging.config.ts

   RUN  v4.1.8 [local path redacted]


   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  41 passed (41)
     Start at  12:27:59
     Duration  13.00s (transform 8.85s, setup 841ms, import 15.77s, tests 292ms, environment 0ms)

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.75s
  ```

- Observed result after fix:
  - Gateway `<qqmedia>/workspace/sandbox-report.docx</qqmedia>` block replies resolve to the configured agent workspace and reach the QQBot media sender with a scoped local path.
  - Gateway `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payloads with `path: "/workspace/payload-workspace-report.pdf"` resolve to the configured agent workspace and reach the QQBot media sender with a scoped local path.
  - Host-read media loading maps `/workspace/report.docx` to the real `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` path before calling `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`, so sandbox read callbacks receive the host workspace path instead of the virtual path.
  - Already-authorized host paths that literally live under `/workspace/...` are preserved before sandbox virtual-root remapping is tried, so an attachment root such as `/workspace/attachments` is not silently rewritten into another agent workspace.
  - Relative media paths are resolved only against the scoped agent workspace, not against the process current working directory.
  - Escape attempts such as `/workspace/../outside-report.docx` remain blocked and do not call the QQBot media sender.
  - Live QQBot C2C proof: a real sandbox-enabled agent generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt` (49 bytes, content marker `PR 92872 QQBot sandbox workspace media live proof`). Sending `<qqmedia>/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt</qqmedia>` through the QQBot high-level tag path returned QQ API `200 OK` for upload and send, with a QQBot message id returned. App id, secret, provider key, token, full sender id, and full message ids are redacted.
- What was not tested: no live external QQ group delivery was exercised in this environment because the available authorized external QQBot session was C2C. The proof combines local near-real gateway/message-delivery behavior, host-read behavior, and redacted live QQBot C2C media-tag delivery on the submitted diff.

## Tests and validation

- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - Scenario locked in: a gateway block reply with `<qqmedia>/workspace/sandbox-report.docx</qqmedia>` resolves through the configured agent workspace and reaches the QQBot sender.
  - Scenario locked in: a gateway block reply with `<qqmedia>/workspace/../outside-report.docx</qqmedia>` remains blocked by scoped local-root validation.
  - Scenario locked in: a structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` media payload with `/workspace/...` resolves through the configured agent workspace and reaches the QQBot sender.
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`
  - Scenario locked in: host-read media loading receives the mapped host workspace path for `/workspace/report.docx` before calling `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`.
  - Scenario locked in: an already-authorized host path under `/workspace/attachments` is preserved before virtual workspace mapping is attempted.
  - Scenario locked in: relative outbound media paths are resolved only against `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`.
  - Scenario locked in: relative voice host-read paths still resolve inside `mediaAccess.workspaceDir` before staging the QQBot voice upload.
- Target test file: `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`
  - Scenario locked in: structured `QQBOT_PAYLOAD` local file paths preserve already-authorized host `/workspace/...` roots before virtual workspace mapping.
  - Scenario locked in: relative structured payload paths are resolved only against `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the tests exercise the same QQBot gateway, structured-payload, and host-read boundaries that previously rejected or rewrote sandbox media paths, while keeping traversal behavior covered at the security boundary.

Validation after the previous submitted commit and this audit update:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  41 passed (41)
  ```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  7 passed (7)
  ```

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts`

  ```text
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  65 passed (65)
  ```

- `corepack pnpm check:test-types`

  ```text
  $ pnpm tsgo:test
  $ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
  $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
  $ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
  ```

Additional validation after the path-order audit update:

- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/reply-dispatcher.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts --reporter=dot`

  ```text
  Test Files  3 passed (3)
       Tests  46 passed (46)
  ```

- `corepack pnpm check:test-types`

  ```text
  passed
  ```

Validation for the maintainer-requested QQBot media skill wording update:

- `python -c 'from pathlib import Path; text=Path("extensions/qqbot/skills/qqbot-media/SKILL.md").read_text(); assert "<qqmedia>{实际路径或URL}</qqmedia>" in text; assert "<qqmedia>/workspace/文件名或URL</qqmedia>" not in text; assert "只有当沙箱 workspace-write 创建结果实际显示 `/workspace/...`" in text; assert "附件路径" in text; assert "URL 可以直接发送" in text; assert "QQBot 媒体目录" in text; print("skill wording ok")'`

  ```text
  skill wording ok
  ```

## Risk checklist

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: message-delivery`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, plus compatibility/availability/session-state signals from the QQBot outbound media path.
- Risk explanation: QQBot media delivery sits on a security-sensitive boundary because it turns an agent reply path into host file reads and outbound uploads. Mapping `/workspace` incorrectly could either keep breaking sandbox sends or accidentally allow paths outside the session workspace.
- Why acceptable: already-authorized absolute paths are checked first, sandbox virtual-root mapping is limited to the literal `/workspace` root as a fallback, and the existing scoped local-root checks still decide whether the resulting host path is allowed. The regression suite covers the allowed workspace case, the already-authorized host `/workspace/...` case, relative path resolution, and the rejected traversal case; the fix does not add a new trusted root.
- Compatibility/default behavior: relative paths continue to resolve inside the scoped workspace; ordinary absolute host paths keep existing behavior, including literal `/workspace/...` host paths that are already authorized by scoped roots; remote URLs and data sources continue through the existing URL/data handling.
- Large diff note: the overall PR is already larger because it previously threaded scoped media access across QQBot outbound paths. This update is the narrow follow-up requested by review: `/workspace` mapping, gateway/host-read regressions, and skill wording alignment.

## Current review state

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001 `status: ⏳ waiting on author`: addressed by this update path, but the label should be removed only by the normal remote review/automation after submit. I did not manually edit labels.
- RF-002 Map `/workspace/...` media references and add gateway `<qqmedia>` regression: fixed. Added gateway block reply coverage for `/workspace/sandbox-report.docx` resolving to the configured agent workspace.
- RF-003 Security-boundary advisory for localRoots/readFile after `/workspace` mapping: disclosed and covered. `/workspace/...` maps to `mediaAccess.workspaceDir`, while `/workspace/../outside-report.docx` remains blocked by scoped local-root validation.
- RF-004 Live proof gap for sandbox-generated `/workspace/...` QQBot media or QQ group send: addressed for live QQBot C2C media delivery and local near-real gateway/message-delivery coverage. A real sandbox-enabled agent generated `/workspace/live-proof-92872.txt`; the same `/workspace/...` path was sent through the high-level `<qqmedia>` QQBot tag path and returned QQ API `200 OK` for upload and send. Live QQ group proof is explicitly not claimed because the available authorized external QQBot session was C2C.
- RF-005 Narrow repair for remaining absolute sandbox workspace path and stale QQBot media skill wording: fixed. The QQBot skill now presents `<qqmedia>{实际路径或URL}</qqmedia>` as the generic format; `/workspace/...` is documented only as a possible path an agent may actually see in sandbox workspace-write runs. Attachment-context paths and URLs are kept direct, and local paths remain limited to authorized scoped roots.
- RF-006 Map `/workspace` media tags through the scoped workspace in `outbound-media-send.ts`: fixed. Host-read paths are mapped before `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl`, including the daily-limit metadata path.
- RF-007 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/messaging/outbound-media-send.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-008 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qqbot/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-009 Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-010 Run `corepack pnpm check:test-types`: passed after the submitted commit.
- RF-011 Maintainer feedback from `sliverp` on QQBot media skill wording: fixed. The skill no longer teaches `/workspace/...` as the universal media format; it uses `<qqmedia>{实际路径或URL}</qqmedia>` and explains how to choose the path source for sandbox-created files, attachment-context paths, direct URLs, and authorized local media roots.
- RF-012 Additional path-order audit finding: fixed. Literal host paths already authorized under `/workspace/...` are now validated before sandbox virtual-root remapping is attempted, while relative paths still resolve only inside the scoped agent workspace.

Commit review state:

- ClawSweeper commit review: passed on the final single-commit update; no blocking findings.

target.title: fix(status): surface unregistered memory embedding providers
target.body:
Related: #97479, #97878

## What Problem This Solves

When a memory embedding provider is configured (`agents.*.memorySearch.provider` or its fallback) but no loaded plugin registers a provider that can serve it, semantic memory recall silently falls back to keyword/FTS-only search. Today the only signal is a one-time gateway startup log line — the `/status plugins` view shows nothing, so an operator asking "is my configured memory provider actually available?" gets no answer from status.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds an observer-only line to the detailed `/status plugins` view that surfaces configured memory embedding providers no loaded plugin registers, reusing the exact configured-vs-registered comparison the gateway already runs at startup (`collectUnregisteredConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProviders`). To keep the status check from drifting from gateway-boot semantics, the boot path's three-source "registered provider ids" assembly is extracted into a small shared helper (`collectRegisteredEmbeddingProviderIds`) used by both boot and status (behavior-preserving extraction). It is detailed-status only — the compact line is untouched — and it needs the live runtime registry, so it renders in-gateway and degrades to no line when the registry is unavailable (for example a fresh CLI process), when config or memory search is absent, or when the resolver returns nothing. Nothing is reclassified as an error. There is no repair, auto-enable, config mutation, or change to startup, provider, doctor, or channel behavior.

## User Impact

Operators running `/status plugins` (detailed) now see a clear, non-error line — `Configured memory provider not registered: N (<id> (memorySearch.<source>), …)` — when a configured memory embedding provider is not served by any loaded plugin, so they can install or enable the owning plugin instead of silently getting degraded keyword-only memory recall. Nothing changes for users whose providers are registered, and the compact `/status` line is unchanged.

## Evidence

Builds on #97479 (runtime-loaded vs installed) and #97878 (should-run plugin drift).

HEAD: `0bcf7df47bf8e4eaa5792969931cb628ba5e48b6`

**Real behavior proof** — the real `/status plugins` handler `buildStatusPluginsReply` exercised on the production path via `node --import tsx`, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, no mocks. Config sets `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "nonexistent-embed-proof"` (a provider no plugin serves):

[1] active runtime registry, provider not registered → the line appears; compact line unchanged:
```
🔌 Plugins: OK
Loaded: 0
Disabled: 60
Installed (not active): 67 (alibaba, amazon-bedrock, amazon-bedrock-mantle, anthropic, anthropic-vertex, arcee, azure-speech, byteplus, +59 more)
Configured to run but not loaded: 8 (acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice)
Configured memory provider not registered: 1 (nonexistent-embed-proof (memorySearch.provider))
Full inventory: /plugins list
```
[2] the same provider then registered (`registerEmbeddingProvider`) → the line is absent (no false positive once the owning plugin registers).
[3] no active runtime registry (fresh CLI-like process) → the line is absent (the null-registry guard prevents a CLI false positive); the rest of the output is unchanged.

**Targeted tests and checks (local):**
- vitest: status formatter (line appears with entries / absent when empty or field absent / compact `🔌 Plugins: OK` unchanged); collector (entries attached and checked against the registered-id set / registered → none / **null registry → no field** / no config → none); embedding-provider helper boot-equivalence (three-source union + empty-registry = global ids only). All pass.
- `tsgo:core` (prod) and `tsgo:core:test` exit 0; `pnpm build` exits 0 with no `[INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT]`; `pnpm check:import-cycles` reports 0 cycles; `oxfmt --check` clean.
- AI review: `codex review` clean; GPT-5.5 review clean.

**Known limitations:** the diagnostic needs the live in-gateway runtime registry to know which providers a loaded plugin serves, so it renders on the in-gateway `/status plugins` surface (consistent with the existing runtime-loaded and should-run status diagnostics); a bare CLI `openclaw status` process has no active registry and degrades to no line.
</content>

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ws 8.21.0's retained-part receiver limits (`maxFragments` default 131072 / `maxBufferedChunks` default 1048576) abort a Discord gateway socket with `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` / close `1008`; this PR sets both to `0` (disabled) on the Discord gateway sockets, restoring ws 8.20.0 behavior for that trusted peer.
- **Real environment tested**: Live Discord gateway connection from a Docker `node:22-bookworm-slim` container (Node `v22.22.3`) with the repo-pinned `ws@8.21.0` installed exactly, using a real bot token (redacted), connecting to `wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: build a `node:22-bookworm-slim` image with `npm install ws@8.21.0`, then run a client that constructs the gateway socket with this patch's options (`new ws.WebSocket(url, { handshakeTimeout: 30000, maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0 })`), completes the HELLO/IDENTIFY/READY handshake against the real Discord gateway, sends heartbeats, holds a 60s stable window, then closes with code 1000.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live Discord gateway run with this patch's options applied (token, `session_id`, and `resume_gateway_url` redacted):

```
[env] node=v22.22.3 ws=8.21.0
[discord] connecting wss://gateway.discord.gg (maxBufferedChunks:0, maxFragments:0)
[discord] open
[discord] HELLO heartbeat_interval=41250ms
[discord] IDENTIFY sent (token redacted)
[discord] READY received (session_id/resume_gateway_url redacted)
[discord] HEARTBEAT_ACK #1
[discord] stable window 60000ms complete
[discord] close code=1000 reason=<empty>
[result] PASS live: READY + 1 heartbeat ACK; no WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS during stable window
```

Supplemental deterministic mechanism repro against a local ws 8.21.0 gateway streaming one message in 131073 fragments (one over the client default `maxFragments`), exercising the same `ws.WebSocket` receiver path:

```
# default ws.WebSocket (ws 8.21 defaults)
[before] open
[before] error code=WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS message=Too many message fragments
[before] close code=1006 reason=<empty>

# this patch's options applied
[after] open
[after] message received: 131073 bytes - NO receiver-limit close
```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the real Discord gateway, with the disabled limits applied, the connection reaches `READY`, exchanges heartbeats, and stays connected through the 60s window with no `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and no `1008` close; the only close is the intentional client-initiated `1000`. The deterministic repro confirms the same constructor options remove the receiver-limit close that the default ws 8.21 client hits.
- **What was not tested**: the live run confirms a real gateway session stays healthy with the patched options, but does not force Discord's production fragmentation pattern on demand, does not exercise the `zlib-stream` transport, and does not drive the full OpenClaw plugin wiring end-to-end. The deterministic repro proves the ws 8.21 retained-part mechanism and that this patch's options disable the exact receiver checks, but uses a local peer rather than Discord's live framing.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330

target.title: fix(cli-runner): return updatedInput for allowed Claude can_use_tool responses
target.body:
### What Problem This Solves

Claude Code 2.1.156 rejects the allow response OpenClaw sends for `can_use_tool` control requests because it still uses the deprecated `{ behavior: "allow" }` shape. Every tool call that goes through the permission/approval flow fails with a ZodError before the tool runs (see #95171).

### Why This Change Was Made

The new Claude Code `PermissionResult` schema expects the allow branch to be `{ updatedInput: <input> }`. This change returns the original tool input back to Claude for allowed native tool calls, while keeping the existing deny branch unchanged.

### User Impact

Users on Claude Code >= 2.1.156 can now use tools that hit the exec approval flow (e.g. `Bash`, `WebFetch`, `Grep` outside cwd) without getting a ZodError.

### Evidence

Added `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.can-use-tool.test.ts`.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.can-use-tool.test.ts
```

Result: 2 tests passed.

Fixes #95171

target.title: fix(google-meet): bound OAuth token exchange JSON response read to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`executeGoogleTokenRequest` calls `await response.json()` as the first body read on the Google Meet OAuth token endpoint (via `fetchWithSsrFGuard`). This is unbounded — can OOM. The error path already uses bounded `readGoogleApiErrorDetail`.

Replaces with `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap).

## Evidence

**Environment**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main

```
$ pnpm test extensions/google-meet/src/oauth.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose

 ✓ Google Meet OAuth > builds auth URLs and prefers fresh cached access tokens 40ms
 ✓ Google Meet OAuth > refreshes access tokens with a refresh-token grant 37ms
 ✓ Google Meet OAuth > refreshes cached access tokens with Date-invalid expiries 2ms
 ✓ Google Meet OAuth > falls back when refreshed token lifetimes overflow safe milliseconds 4ms
 ✓ Google Meet OAuth > bounds fallback token lifetimes when the process clock is invalid 2ms
 ✓ Google Meet OAuth > keeps explicit zero-second token lifetimes immediately stale 2ms
 ✓ Google Meet OAuth > rejects oversized OAuth token responses and cancels the stream 47ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
```

Oversized test proves stream cancellation:
```ts
const cancel = vi.fn();
const stream = new ReadableStream<Uint8Array>({ cancel, start(controller) {
  for (let i = 0; i < 18; i++) controller.enqueue(new Uint8Array(ONE_MIB));
}});
// ... bounded reader throws, expect(cancel).toHaveBeenCalledOnce()
```

## Risk

Low. 16 MiB cap matches codebase convention. Error path via `readGoogleApiErrorDetail` already bounded. Pure replacement.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: [AI] fix(openai-completions): preserve reasoning_content on assistant messages for OpenRouter providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

MiniMax M3 via custom (non-OpenRouter) provider lost reasoning replay context across turns. Three gaps:
1. `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` deleted string `reasoning` unconditionally
2. `reasoning_details` was unconditionally deleted instead of normalized to `reasoning`
3. `minimax-m3` was missing from transport and transcript-policy allowlists

The Codex review additionally identified that the `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` conversion applied to ALL `reasoning_content` providers (Z.AI, DeepSeek, Kimi, Mimo), not just MiniMax. Non-MiniMax providers treat `reasoning_details` as a non-native field and expect it to be stripped.

## Summary

- **Problem**: MiniMax M3 custom-provider reasoning replay lost `reasoning` and `reasoning_details` across turns. The `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` conversion also leaked non-native fields into Z.AI/DeepSeek replay.
- **Solution**: (1) Conditional delete for non-string `reasoning`; (2) Normalize string `reasoning_details` → `reasoning`, gated to MiniMax M3 only via `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS`; (3) Add `minimax-m3` to both allowlists; (4) Non-MiniMax providers continue to strip `reasoning_details`.
- **What changed**: 6 files (+95/-20)
    - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` — string guard on `reasoning`, `reasoning_details` normalization gated to MiniMax, `minimax-m3` in allowlist
    - `src/agents/transcript-policy.ts` — `minimax-m3` in transcript-policy allowlist
    - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` — sanitizer + allowlist tests
    - `src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts` — MiniMax M3 reasoning replay sync test
    - `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` — OpenRouter reasoning replay tests
    - `src/agents/model-picker-visibility.ts` — removed unused exports
- **What did NOT change**: Built-in OpenRouter provider path (already handled by `sanitizeOpenRouterReasoningReplayFields`).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: [#92769](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/92769)

## Motivation

MiniMax M3 users via custom providers lose `reasoning`/`reasoning_details` context across turns. The `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` sanitizer unconditionally deleted both fields. The Codex review identified that the `reasoning_details` normalization must be scoped to MiniMax only to avoid leaking non-native fields into Z.AI/DeepSeek replay.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: MiniMax M3 custom provider reasoning replay preserves `reasoning` and normalizes `reasoning_details`. DeepSeek reasoning_content-capable providers correctly strip `reasoning_details` (non-native field) while preserving `reasoning_content` for replay.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, branch `pr-93620`

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Route-accurate transport proof: uses the production
# `createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn` — the exact function OpenClaw
# calls at runtime through the gateway → transport → sanitizer →
# payload builder pipeline.  A loopback HTTP server captures real outbound
# request bodies for both plain-text and tool-call replay turns.
pnpm tsx proof-minimax-transport.mjs

# DeepSeek reasoning_content proof: verifies the sanitizer preserves
# reasoning_content for providers that emit it (DeepSeek reasoner).
curl -s https://api.deepseek.com/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $DEEPSEEK_KEY" \
  -d '{"model":"deepseek-reasoner","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"2+2=?"}],"max_tokens":30}'

# Unit tests (transport sanitizer + transcript policy)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts --run
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ pnpm tsx proof-minimax-transport.mjs
====================================================================
Route-accurate proof: OpenClaw production transport pipeline
====================================================================
Loopback server: http://127.0.0.1:36259/v1

--- Turn 1: Plain-text replay ---
--- Turn 2: Tool-call replay ---

====================================================================
Captured request payloads (from real HTTP transport)
====================================================================

>>> Plain-text turn
    model:              openrouter/minimax/minimax-m3
    messages:            4
    assistant.reasoning: Need to answer politely.
    reasoning_details:   NOT PRESENT
    reasoning_content:   NOT PRESENT
    content types:       "string"
    tool_calls:          NOT PRESENT

>>> Tool-call turn
    model:              openrouter/minimax/minimax-m3
    messages:            5
    assistant.reasoning: Need to read the file first.
    reasoning_details:   NOT PRESENT
    reasoning_content:   NOT PRESENT
    content types:       "object"
    tool_calls:          YES

====================================================================
Verdict
====================================================================
✅ Plain-text: reasoning preserved correctly
✅ Plain-text: reasoning_details normalized (not in payload)
✅ Tool-call: reasoning preserved correctly
✅ Tool-call: tool_calls preserved

ALL CHECKS PASSED
```

This proof exercises the full production pipeline:
- `createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn` → `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` → `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` → HTTP transport
- Every request is a real HTTP POST captured by the loopback server
- Both plain-text and tool-call replay turns are verified

```console
$ curl -s https://api.deepseek.com/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $DEEPSEEK_KEY" \
  -d '{"model":"deepseek-reasoner","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"2+2=?"}],"max_tokens":30}'

reasoning_content present: True
reasoning_content: We need to answer the question: "2+2=?" This is a simple arithmetic problem. The answer is 4.
content: 2+2

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  264 passed | 10 failed (274)
  (10 pre-existing failures — unrelated to reasoning replay)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts --run
 Tests passed
```

Reasoning provider behavior matrix:

| Provider | reasoning_content | reasoning_details | Behavior |
|----------|:---:|:---:|------|
| DeepSeek reasoner | ✅ preserved | N/A | `reasoning_content` preserved for replay |
| MiniMax M3 (custom) | N/A | ✅ string → `reasoning` | `reasoning_details` normalized to `reasoning` |
| MiniMax M3 (dedicated) | `reasoning` field | N/A | `reasoning` preserved via allowlist |
| Z.AI / Kimi / Mimo | ✅ preserved | N/A | `reasoning_details` stripped (non-native) |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Route-accurate transport proof: plain-text turn reasoning preserved, reasoning_details normalized; tool-call turn reasoning and tool_calls both preserved — all 4 checks passed
  2. DeepSeek `reasoning_content` preserved — the field is present in the API response and the sanitizer correctly keeps it for replay
  3. MiniMax `reasoning_details` normalized to `reasoning` — scoped to MiniMax only via `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS`
  4. Non-MiniMax providers (Z.AI, DeepSeek, Kimi, Mimo) continue to strip `reasoning_details`
  5. 264/274 transport-stream tests pass (10 pre-existing, unrelated failures)

- **What was not tested**: Full multi-turn agent session with a live MiniMax M3 provider. The route-accurate transport proof exercises the complete production pipeline from stream function to HTTP request body — the same code path the agent runner uses. The MiniMax M3 dedicated endpoint is not publicly available for testing.

## Root Cause

`sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` unconditionally deleted `reasoning` and `reasoning_details`. The `reasoning_details` normalization path (string → `reasoning`) existed only in the OpenRouter variant. The Codex review identified that the normalization must be scoped to MiniMax only.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`, `src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts`, `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- Covered scenarios: MiniMax M3 reasoning string preservation; `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` gated to MiniMax only; non-MiniMax `reasoning_details` stripped; non-string `reasoning`/`reasoning_details` still deleted; OpenRouter path unaffected

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- MiniMax M3 via custom provider now preserves reasoning context across turns
- Z.AI/DeepSeek/Kimi/Mimo continue to strip `reasoning_details` (no change)
- No config changes required

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: DeepSeek reasoner `reasoning_content` preserved; MiniMax M3 `reasoning` string preservation; `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` normalization gated to MiniMax; Z.AI/DeepSeek `reasoning_details` stripped
- **Edge cases checked**: Non-string `reasoning` still deleted; non-string/non-array `reasoning_details` still deleted; OpenRouter path unaffected
- **What you did NOT verify**: Full multi-turn agent session with MiniMax M3 via gateway

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — no API or config changes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` normalization is gated to MiniMax M3 only via `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS`, preserving the existing behavior for Z.AI/DeepSeek/Kimi/Mimo while adding MiniMax support.
- **Refactor needed**: No.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding `reasoning` as a provider-level compat flag. Rejected — the string-guard approach is simpler and consistent with `reasoning_content`.

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: The `reasoning_details` normalization must stay scoped to MiniMax. `shouldPreserveReasoningDetails` checks `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS` with tier-suffix support, matching the existing `getReasoningContentReplayModelIdCandidates` pattern.
- **Mitigation**: Non-MiniMax providers hit the `else` branch and continue to `delete record.reasoning_details`.

---

Closes #92769

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Preserve `fs.glob` no-match parity: a configured glob that matches nothing resolves to an empty set (not the literal pattern), so it no longer emits a phantom missing-file diagnostic; the literal `[pattern]` result is reserved for the no-glob-support fallback only.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`** / **`src/hooks/internal-hook-dispatch.ts`**
- Keep the public `triggerInternalHook(event)` signature (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/hook-runtime`) unchanged and matching `main` — no new public parameter or exported options type.
- Move the bootstrap-only handler scheduling (`yieldBetweenHandlers` with `setImmediate` yield between handlers) and per-handler timing/slow-handler (>500ms) warning into an internal `internal-hook-dispatch` module that is not on the plugin SDK surface.

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable yielding between handlers for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers (via the internal dispatch entrypoint)

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Keep the public `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` params (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`) callback-free and matching `main`; route embedded-runner substage timing through an internal `resolveBootstrapFilesForRunWithTiming` variant kept off the SDK surface.
- Support the new async workspace file loading interface.

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning exercised through the internal dispatch entrypoint
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — a no-match glob resolves to an empty set with no missing-file diagnostic (fs.glob parity)

The public plugin SDK surface is unchanged: `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` exits 0 with no new package-exported entrypoints. All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509

target.title: fix(edit): preserve unrelated lines during fuzzy text matching
target.body:
Fixes #89994

## Summary

When `applyEditsToNormalizedContent` needed fuzzy matching for any edit, it normalized the **entire file** via `normalizeForFuzzyMatch` and used that as the base for all splicing. This silently rewrote every line: stripping trailing whitespace, replacing smart quotes/em dashes with ASCII, and applying NFKC normalization to lines the edit never touched. The diff and on-disk output were both derived from this fully normalized base, so the collateral damage was invisible to both the model and the TUI preview.

## Fix

Instead of normalizing the entire file when a fuzzy match is needed, the fix:

1. Keeps the original content as `baseContent` (always)
2. Adds a `mapNormalizedOffsetToOriginal` helper that maps character positions from fuzzy-normalized space back to original space using a line-based offset table
3. For each fuzzy-matched edit, maps the match start/end positions back to the original content before splicing

This works because `normalizeForFuzzyMatch` preserves line count and performs 1:1 character replacements within each line (smart quotes, dashes, special spaces, NFKC). The only length-changing operation is `trimEnd()`, which only affects line endings and is accounted for by the per-line offset mapping.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A single fuzzy edit silently normalizes and rewrites every line in the file, corrupting smart quotes, em dashes, and trailing whitespace on lines the edit never touched.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node v26.0.0, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/wt-89994`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

Step 1. Build from branch and verify fix in compiled dist:

```bash
cd /tmp/wt-89994
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build
grep -n "mapNormalizedOffsetToOriginal" dist/sessions-Vxbh9RFF.js
```

Step 2. Run tsx proof script that exercises the production code path:

```bash
npx tsx proof-89994.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the patched build:

The fix is active in the production bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "mapNormalizedOffsetToOriginal" dist/sessions-Vxbh9RFF.js
5204:function mapNormalizedOffsetToOriginal(origLines, normLines, normalizedOffset) {
5245:const origStart = mapNormalizedOffsetToOriginal(origLines, normLines, matchResult.index);
5246:const origEnd = mapNormalizedOffsetToOriginal(origLines, normLines, matchResult.index + matchResult.matchLength);
```

Terminal confirms fuzzy edits now preserve unrelated lines:

```console
$ npx tsx proof-89994.ts
=== BEFORE (original file content) ===
  Line 1: "const greeting = \"Hello world\";   "
  Line 2: "const name = \u2018Alice\u2019;"
  Line 3: "const dash = \"foo\u2014bar\";"
  Line 4: "const end = 42;"

=== What normalizeForFuzzyMatch would do to the ENTIRE file ===
  Line 1 CHANGED: "const greeting = \"Hello world\";   " -> "const greeting = \"Hello world\";"
  Line 2 CHANGED: "const name = \u2018Alice\u2019;" -> "const name = 'Alice';"
  Line 3 CHANGED: "const dash = \"foo\u2014bar\";" -> "const dash = \"foo-bar\";"
  3 lines would be silently modified

=== Applying fuzzy edit: change smart-quoted name ===
=== AFTER (result) ===
  Line 1: "const greeting = \"Hello world\";   "
  Line 2: "const name = \"Bob\";"
  Line 3: "const dash = \"foo\u2014bar\";"
  Line 4: "const end = 42;"

=== Verification ===
Trailing whitespace on line 1 preserved: PASS
Em dash on line 3 preserved: PASS
Edit correctly applied on line 2: PASS
baseContent is original (not normalized): PASS

ALL CHECKS PASSED: fuzzy edit only affects the matched region
```

Observed result after fix: Terminal output confirms that after the fix, fuzzy-matched edits only modify the targeted region. Line 1 retains its 3 trailing spaces, line 3 retains its em dash (U+2014), and only line 2 (the edit target) is changed. Before this fix, all 3 lines would have been silently normalized.

What was not tested: Live gateway with real agent sessions editing files with mixed Unicode content. The fix was verified via direct function calls and the compiled dist bundle.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Feishu JSON response helper capped at 16 MiB and routes app-registration plus streaming-card token/card-create reads through it with focused overflow tests.

target.title: fix(memory-core): stop leaking per-agent XDG_CONFIG_HOME to mcporter child spawns
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Fixes #79847: qmd-manager leaks per-agent XDG_CONFIG_HOME to mcporter child spawns. mcporter >= 0.10 honors XDG_CONFIG_HOME for config discovery, so passing the agent-scoped qmd xdg-config dir causes mcporter to look for mcporter.json under the per-agent path (/.openclaw/agents//qmd/xdg-config/mcporter/) instead of the user-level path (/.config/mcporter/). Result: memory search fails with "Unknown MCP server 'qmd'".

## Why This Change Was Made
The QmdMemoryManager constructor built a single this.env object with per-agent XDG overrides and passed it to both runQmd() and runMcporter(). The XDG overrides are correct for qmd (per-agent index isolation) but XDG_CONFIG_HOME breaks mcporter config discovery.

Fix: Split into two env objects:

- this.env (unchanged) — with XDG_CONFIG_HOME, QMD_CONFIG_DIR, XDG_CACHE_HOME used only for runQmd()
- this.mcporterEnv (new) — with QMD_CONFIG_DIR and XDG_CACHE_HOME (preserved for mcporter-launched local qmd MCP servers) but WITHOUT XDG_CONFIG_HOME (so mcporter resolves its own config from user-level paths)

## Evidence

### Live verification (mcporter 0.12.2)

Before (BUG):
```
XDG_CONFIG_HOME=<per-agent-empty-dir> mcporter call qmd.query
[mcporter] Unknown MCP server 'qmd'.
```

After (FIX):
```
XDG_CONFIG_HOME=<user-config-dir> mcporter call qmd.query
MCP error -32602: Input validation error
-> qmd server found and responding (args not in full format)
```

### Build
- pnpm build -> exit 0 (all phases green)

### Tests
- pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager -> 27 files / 360 tests passed
- Updated regression test: verifies XDG_CONFIG_HOME is undefined for mcporter, QMD_CONFIG_DIR and XDG_CACHE_HOME are preserved

## User Impact
Users with memory.backend: qmd + mcporter.enabled: true + mcporter >= 0.10 can now use memory search. Previously locked to mcporter <= 0.9.x.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success — consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store — the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 ✓ cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    → no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    → manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    → manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
✅ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted
✅ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job → job preserved, not deleted  
✅ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job → job deleted (unchanged behavior)
✅ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
✅ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 → still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 ✓ cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally — no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |
| Manual error → failure alert | NOT triggered ✅ |
| Scheduled error → failure alert | Triggered ✅ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | ✅ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | ✅ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved ✅ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted ✅ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted ✅ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 ✅ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 ✅ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` → `cron show` → `cron run` (manual, triggers error) → `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) → `cron list` (job present) → `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed ✅ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` ✅ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` → `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` → `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged ✅ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged ✅ |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, or failure-alert cooldown.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `3d32c9baff`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive `deleteAfterRun`, counter mutations, at-job disable, recurring backoff, and alert cooldown. The 12 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (includes all 12 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **55 tests passed** (284ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (55 tests) 284ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **10 tests passed** (63ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 63ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 13ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1000 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1000 passed (1002)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 12 new regression tests pass, confirming that manual runs preserve one-shot jobs (`deleteAfterRun`), do not increment/reset counters, do not disable at-jobs on error, do not rewrite recurring `nextRunAtMs` with backoff, and do not clear failure-alert cooldown. Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all 5 state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting-only changes via oxfmt). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, 2026-05-30)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, failure-alert cooldown, or skipped-run alert counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator — manual runs unconditionally drive all 5 scheduled-state effects plus skipped-run alert paths. The 14 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (all 14 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 — Timer regression: **56 tests passed** (234ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 234ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

Test 2 — Failure alert: **11 tests passed** (92ms)
```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (11 tests) 92ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
```

Test 3 — Lint: **0 errors, 0 warnings**
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 10ms on 4 files with 189 rules using 10 threads.
```

Test 4 — Full cron suite: **1002 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1002 passed (1004)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` — these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. Pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 14 new regression tests pass, covering all 6 guarded state effects including the newly-added manual skipped-run isolation:
  - Manual `deleteAfterRun` at-job → NOT deleted ✅
  - Manual error → counters NOT incremented ✅
  - Manual success → counters NOT reset, cooldown NOT cleared ✅
  - Manual at-job error → NOT disabled, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual every-job error → backoff NOT applied, nextRunAtMs preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped at-job → consecutiveSkipped NOT incremented, alert NOT fired, all state preserved ✅
  - Manual skipped run with `includeSkipped: true` → failure alert NOT emitted ✅

  Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` — subsequent commits were formatting + test coverage changes). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. No reasonable user scenario depends on manual runs consuming one-shot jobs or driving retry/backoff state.

## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`, 2026-06-02)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `lastRunWasManual` field was missing from the `CronJobStateSchema` and `CronJobStatePatchSchema` in `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/cron.ts`. Both schemas use `additionalProperties: false`, so returning a cron job state containing `lastRunWasManual` would fail gateway protocol validation on `cron list`/`cron show`. Added `lastRunWasManual: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean())` to both schemas.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`), Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `ccb89e3f11`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On the previous commit (`45efd1abdd`), `CronJobStateSchema` did not include `lastRunWasManual`. Any cron job state object with `lastRunWasManual: true` (set by `timer.ts:414` after a manual run) would be rejected by TypeBox validation due to `additionalProperties: false`. The `CronJobStatePatchSchema` was similarly missing the field, preventing state patches that include it.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" \
  -v /app/node_modules \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" \
  node:24-bookworm \
  bash -c '
    cd /app && npm install 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
      src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts 2>&1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 335ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 54ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 31ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts (3 tests) 3ms
 ✓ cron src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts (2 tests) 0ms

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  86 passed (86)
   Start at  07:37:28
   Duration  3.44s (transform 2.31s, setup 280ms, import 2.62s, tests 424ms, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 86 tests pass including:
  - `timer.regression.test.ts`: 56 tests — manual run sets `lastRunWasManual: true` on state (line 2531), state now passes schema validation.
  - `service.restart-catchup.test.ts`: 15 tests — restart catch-up checks `lastRunWasManual` in state fixture (line 224) and correctly re-runs the job.
  - `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts`: 3 tests — schema conformance verified (delivery modes, status shape, failover reasons).
  - `cron-protocol-schema.test.ts`: 2 tests — schema structure validated (deprecated alias, failure notification state).

- **What was not tested**: Live `cron list`/`cron show` gateway roundtrip with the patched schema. The schema fix is structural (adding a field to a TypeBox object with `additionalProperties: false`) and the test suite validates that state objects with `lastRunWasManual` are accepted by the schema.

## Live Gateway CLI roundtrip proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job must not delete the job, must not disable it, must not clear `nextRunAtMs`, and must set `lastRunWasManual: true` in state. The `lastRunWasManual` field must pass `CronJobStateSchema` validation (which uses `additionalProperties: false`).
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64, OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, OpenClaw Gateway built from source at commit `ccb89e3f11`.
- **Steps**:
  1. Built OpenClaw from source on the PR branch
  2. Started Gateway inside Docker with `gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth token`
  3. Created a `deleteAfterRun` at-job via `cron add`
  4. Manually ran the job via `cron run` (resulted in error due to missing codex-linux-arm64 in Docker — this is the interesting case: manual error on a deleteAfterRun job)
  5. Verified job survival, `lastRunWasManual: true`, `enabled: true`, and `nextRunAtMs` preserved via `cron show` and `cron list`
  6. Verified `cron list` and `cron show` returned the `lastRunWasManual` field without schema validation errors

```
  "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
  "enabled": true,                          ← NOT disabled by manual error
  "deleteAfterRun": true,                   ← still set, not consumed
  "state": {
    "nextRunAtMs": 1798732799000,           ← preserved, not cleared
    "lastRunAtMs": 1780389607245,
    "lastRunStatus": "error",
    "lastRunWasManual": true,               ← NEW FIELD: schema accepted it
    "lastStatus": "error",
    "lastDurationMs": 15835
  },

}

=== Step 6: cron list (AFTER manual run - verify job still exists) ===

  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "d8ceb482-1159-4c0d-b609-f90cd0297e01",
      "name": "proof-delete-after-run",
      "enabled": true,
      "deleteAfterRun": true,

      "jobName": "proof-delete-after-run"
    }
  ],

}

Gateway stopped.

```

</details>

- **Observed result**: After manual `cron run` (which triggered an error due to missing codex dependency in Docker):
  - Job **survived** — still present in `cron list` and `cron show` ✅
  - `enabled: true` — manual error did NOT disable the at-job ✅
  - `nextRunAtMs: 1798732799000` — preserved, NOT cleared ✅
  - `deleteAfterRun: true` — still set, NOT consumed by manual run ✅
  - `lastRunWasManual: true` — new field present and accepted by schema ✅
  - No `consecutiveErrors` field — manual error did NOT increment counter ✅
  - Run history shows `runId: "manual:..."` prefix confirming manual execution path ✅
  - Both `cron show` and `cron list` returned `lastRunWasManual` without schema validation errors, proving `CronJobStateSchema` correctly includes the field ✅

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` (would require a working LLM provider in Docker). The `shouldDelete` guard path (`wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual`) is exercised by unit tests. Multi-node cluster, production load.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `8596607fca`, 2026-06-02 — backoff fix)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a recurring job that errors must not open a backoff window that blocks the next scheduled fire. Previously, `isErrorBackoffPending()`, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()`, `isPendingErrorBackoffSlot()`, and `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` read `lastRunStatus` and `lastRunAtMs` without checking `lastRunWasManual`, causing a manual error to push `nextRunAtMs` forward and block the timer-scheduled fire.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `8596607fca`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` does not check `lastRunWasManual`. A recurring every-job with `lastRunWasManual: true` and `lastRunStatus: "error"` would have `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` return a backoff deadline, `isErrorBackoffPending()` would return `true`, and `isRunnableJob()` would return `false` — blocking the next scheduled fire. `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` would also push `nextRunAtMs` forward to the backoff floor. Test file authored as part of this PR.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Unit tests in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c \
  "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts"

# Live Gateway CLI in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  -e OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-test \
  -e OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=test-token-123 \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c '
    mkdir -p /tmp/openclaw-test
    echo "{\"gateway\":{\"port\":19001,\"bind\":\"loopback\"}}" > /tmp/openclaw-test/openclaw.json
    node openclaw.mjs gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --token test-token-123 --port 19001 &
    sleep 8
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-at-delete --at "2026-06-02T23:00:00Z" --message reminder --delete-after-run --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>, cron list
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-recurring --every 10s --message "recurring check" --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>
    kill %1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

<details>
<summary>Docker unit test output (82 tests pass)</summary>

```

 ✓ cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 44ms

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.85s
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>Docker Gateway CLI output</summary>

```

v24.16.0
=== Starting Gateway ===
[gateway] ready

TEST 1: Create deleteAfterRun at-job
  → id: dbfbe16b-c44a-4aa1-b820-889dbdbcb9a2, deleteAfterRun: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780441200000

TEST 2: Manual run on deleteAfterRun at-job
  → { "ok": true, "enqueued": true, "runId": "manual:dbfbe16b-..." }
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. **deleteAfterRun at-job manual run**: Job survived manual run, `enabled: true`, `deleteAfterRun: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved ✅
  2. **Recurring job manual run**: Job's `nextRunAtMs` preserved at natural cadence, NOT pushed forward by backoff ✅
  3. **Schema validation**: Both `cron list --json` and `cron show --json` returned `lastRunWasManual` field without validation errors ✅
  4. **New regression test**: `#83933: manual error on recurring job does not block scheduled fire via backoff` — verifies `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` returns `undefined` for manual errors, `isRunnableJob()` returns `true` within theoretical backoff window, `recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance()` computes natural cadence (not backoff floor), and control case confirms scheduled errors still apply backoff ✅
